zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Catherine Clifton於1425年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓時，她的父親Sir Gervase Clifton29歲，母親Isabel Francis38歲。她於大約1466年與Thomas Blount結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1469年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾梅爾敦 羅斯享年44歲。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Catherine Clifton was born in 1425, in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Gervase Clifton, was 29 and her mother, Isabel Francis, was 38. She married Thomas Blount about 1466. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1469, in Melton Ross, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44.

Elle a épousé Thomas Blount environ 1466. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1469, à Melton Ross, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Catherine Clifton(이)가 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Gervase Clifton(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Francis(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 대략 1466년 Thomas Blount(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1469년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 멜턴 로스에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.
